scores.brief_assessment: An exceptionally well-structured reference covering every aspect of creating a PyMongo Grove example — directory layout, file patterns, test boilerplate, comparison API, and CLI commands — with near-zero common-knowledge filler, all drawn from proprietary internal conventions an agent cannot infer from training data. The only minor inefficiency is the verbose `setUpClass` error strings in the test template, which could be compressed without loss of utility.
scores.novel_info: The most novel details are the internal `utils.comparison.Expect` API (its specific method names, chaining conventions, and ellipsis-pattern matching syntax such as `"prefix..."` and standalone `...`) and the `snip.js` snippet-extraction tool's marker syntax (`# :snippet-start:`, `# :remove:`, etc.), output path structure (`content/code-examples/tested/python/pymongo/…`), and generated filename format (`your_example.snippet.your-snippet-name.py`). A reviewer should verify these against the actual source code in `code-example-tests/python/pymongo/utils/comparison/` and `snip.js`, since none of these are publicly documented libraries or standard Python/Node.js conventions.
